title: Dryopteris × chrysocarpa
taxonomy: Dryopteris × chrysocarpa is a nothospecies, which is a hybrid between two species of the genus Dryopteris. It belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae and the order Polypodiales. The hybrid is a result of a cross between Dryopteris celsa and Dryopteris crenata. As a hybrid, it is not given a binomial nomenclature following the rules of botanical nomenclature. However, its hybrid name reflects its parent species.
uses: Dryopteris × chrysocarpa is a hybrid species of fern that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's fronds have been used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In some cultures, the plant is also used as an anti-inflammatory agent and to support the immune system. The leaves of the fern are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits and risks of using Dryopteris × chrysocarpa as a medicinal plant.
benefits: Dryopteris × chrysocarpa, also known as the gold-fruited lady fern, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, it is thought to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which could contribute to its potential in treating infections and promoting overall well-being. parts: Dryopteris × chrysocarpa, commonly known as the hybrid autumn fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. preparations: Dryopteris × chrysocarpa is a hybrid species of the Dryopteris genus, and like other ferns in this family, it has been used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties. The plant's fronds and roots are used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. intro: Dryopteris × chrysocarpa, also known as the Chrysocarpus Wood Fern, is a hybrid species of fern that belongs to the genus Dryopteris, characterized by its distinctive fronds and unique reproductive structures. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris × chrysocarpa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
